notes: BLIND single-company menu for BLMN (Bloomin' Brands): 67 events reviewed in order (53 filings + 13 transcripts + 1 stub) plus 16 fiscal.ai KPIs; all 16 KPI labels rewritten into standard driver_names.
Brand-level metrics (Outback, Carrabba's, Bonefish Grill, Fleming's, Aussie Grill, Outback Brazil) kept as separate drivers from company-wide forms per ontology R9.

Skipped/folded (13): cybersecurity risk-factor boilerplate; ESG/sustainability-reporting compliance boilerplate; generic laws/regulations+litigation boilerplate; supplier concentration & supplier freight charges folded into commodity_cost; marketing-spend commentary folded into advertising_expense; Q3-2023 lease termination gain (one-off); Easter/spring-break holiday shifts folded into holiday_season_sales/fiscal_calendar_shift; Ziosk tabletop-device discussion (peripheral); bare 'operating costs' category (covered by labor/utilities/insurance drivers); Hong Kong nine-restaurant closure folded into restaurant_closure; Brazil equity-method earnings folded into refranchising; catering/Carrabba's Bistro folded into off_premises_sales; health-pandemic risk-factor boilerplate (2023 10-Qs).
~30 events were administrative-only (Item 5.02 officer changes, 5.07 votes, Reg FD item lists) with no substantive text; they yielded no candidates.

Borderline names flagged for reconcile: fiscal_calendar_shift and menu_simplification (event-class nouns argued reusable like government_shutdown); aussie_three_course is the literal product name (Aussie 3-Course offering); convertible_notes names the instrument (state=retired kept out of name).
eps_surprise/revenue_surprise grounded in 'above expectations' wording (company-expectations mechanism; consensus never quoted in sources).
